title: spontaneous reboot on resume with 2.6.27
description:
Latest working kernel version: 2.6.26.5
Earliest failing kernel version: 2.6.27-rc2 (haven't tested earlier versions)
Distribution: Debian unstable
Hardware Environment: MSI AMD Socket 754 with VIA K8T800 and AMI BIOS
Software Environment:
Problem Description: Resume from S3 causes machine to reset itself.  

Steps to reproduce:
Enter S3 with "echo mem > /sys/power/state"
Try to resume.
patch:
---
 arch/x86/kernel/acpi/realmode/wakeup.S |    1 +
 1 file changed, 1 insertion(+)

Index: linux-2.6/arch/x86/kernel/acpi/realmode/wakeup.S
===================================================================
--- linux-2.6.orig/arch/x86/kernel/acpi/realmode/wakeup.S
+++ linux-2.6/arch/x86/kernel/acpi/realmode/wakeup.S
@@ -112,6 +112,7 @@ _start:
 	movl	%eax, %cr0
 	jmp	pmode_return
 #else
+	jmp	bogus_real_magic
 	pushw	$0
 	pushw	trampoline_segment
 	pushw	$0
---
 arch/x86/kernel/acpi/wakeup_64.S |    3 ++-
 1 file changed, 2 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)

Index: linux-2.6/arch/x86/kernel/acpi/wakeup_64.S
===================================================================
--- linux-2.6.orig/arch/x86/kernel/acpi/wakeup_64.S
+++ linux-2.6/arch/x86/kernel/acpi/wakeup_64.S
@@ -17,7 +17,7 @@ wakeup_long64:
 	movq	saved_magic, %rax
 	movq	$0x123456789abcdef0, %rdx
 	cmpq	%rdx, %rax
-	jne	bogus_64_magic
+	jmp	bogus_64_magic
 
 	movw	$__KERNEL_DS, %ax
 	movw	%ax, %ss	
@@ -36,6 +36,7 @@ wakeup_long64:
 	jmp	*%rax
 
 bogus_64_magic:
+	hlt
 	jmp	bogus_64_magic
 
 	.align 2
---
 arch/x86/kernel/acpi/sleep.c |   11 ++++++++++-
 1 file changed, 10 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)

Index: linux-2.6/arch/x86/kernel/acpi/sleep.c
===================================================================
--- linux-2.6.orig/arch/x86/kernel/acpi/sleep.c
+++ linux-2.6/arch/x86/kernel/acpi/sleep.c
@@ -36,6 +36,9 @@ int acpi_save_state_mem(void)
 {
 	struct wakeup_header *header;
 	u16 *wakeup_seg;
+#ifdef CONFIG_64BIT
+	unsigned long trampoline_physaddr;
+#endif
 
 	if (!acpi_realmode) {
 		printk(KERN_ERR "Could not allocate memory during boot, "
@@ -97,7 +100,13 @@ int acpi_save_state_mem(void)
 	header->pmode_cr3 = (u32)(swsusp_pg_dir - __PAGE_OFFSET);
 	saved_magic = 0x12345678;
 #else /* CONFIG_64BIT */
-	header->trampoline_segment = setup_trampoline() >> 4;
+	trampoline_physaddr = setup_trampoline();
+	if (trampoline_physaddr != TRAMPOLINE_BASE) {
+		printk(KERN_ERR "PM: Invalid trampoline address: 0x%lx\n",
+			trampoline_physaddr);
+		return -EINVAL;
+	}
+	header->trampoline_segment = trampoline_physaddr >> 4;
 #ifdef CONFIG_SMP
 	stack_start.sp = temp_stack + 4096;
 #endif
---
 arch/x86/kernel/trampoline_64.S |    1 +
 1 file changed, 1 insertion(+)

Index: linux-2.6/arch/x86/kernel/trampoline_64.S
===================================================================
--- linux-2.6.orig/arch/x86/kernel/trampoline_64.S
+++ linux-2.6/arch/x86/kernel/trampoline_64.S
@@ -43,6 +43,7 @@ r_base = .
 	mov	%ax, %es
 	mov	%ax, %ss
 
+	jmp	no_longmode
 
 	movl	$0xA5A5A5A5, trampoline_data - r_base
 				# write marker for master knows we're running
---
 arch/x86/kernel/trampoline_64.S |    4 ++++
 1 file changed, 4 insertions(+)

Index: linux-2.6/arch/x86/kernel/trampoline_64.S
===================================================================
--- linux-2.6.orig/arch/x86/kernel/trampoline_64.S
+++ linux-2.6/arch/x86/kernel/trampoline_64.S
@@ -86,6 +86,10 @@ startup_32:
 	movl	$__KERNEL_DS, %eax	# Initialize the %ds segment register
 	movl	%eax, %ds
 
+1:
+	hlt
+	jmp	1b
+
 	xorl	%eax, %eax
 	btsl	$5, %eax		# Enable PAE mode
 	movl	%eax, %cr4
---
 arch/x86/kernel/trampoline_64.S |    3 +++
 1 file changed, 3 insertions(+)

Index: linux-2.6/arch/x86/kernel/trampoline_64.S
===================================================================
--- linux-2.6.orig/arch/x86/kernel/trampoline_64.S
+++ linux-2.6/arch/x86/kernel/trampoline_64.S
@@ -115,6 +115,9 @@ startup_32:
 	.code64
 	.balign 4
 startup_64:
+	hlt
+	jmp	startup_64
+
 	# Now jump into the kernel using virtual addresses
 	movq	$secondary_startup_64, %rax
 	jmp	*%rax
---
 arch/x86/kernel/acpi/sleep.c |    3 +++
 1 file changed, 3 insertions(+)

Index: linux-2.6/arch/x86/kernel/acpi/sleep.c
===================================================================
--- linux-2.6.orig/arch/x86/kernel/acpi/sleep.c
+++ linux-2.6/arch/x86/kernel/acpi/sleep.c
@@ -10,6 +10,7 @@
 #include <linux/dmi.h>
 #include <linux/cpumask.h>
 #include <asm/segment.h>
+#include <asm/desc.h>
 
 #include "realmode/wakeup.h"
 #include "sleep.h"
@@ -100,6 +101,8 @@ int acpi_save_state_mem(void)
 	header->trampoline_segment = setup_trampoline() >> 4;
 #ifdef CONFIG_SMP
 	stack_start.sp = temp_stack + 4096;
+	early_gdt_descr.address =
+			(unsigned long)get_cpu_gdt_table(smp_processor_id());
 #endif
 	initial_code = (unsigned long)wakeup_long64;
 	saved_magic = 0x123456789abcdef0;
